_decision.title: B4-v3: 5-task complex-task suite for two-phase hypothesis matrix
_decision.rationale: Issue #644 (WI-B4-V3-HYPOTHESIS-MATRIX) requires tasks where Haiku-unhooked fails ≥50% (quality-delta requirement). Tasks selected from operator-suggested domains: (1) json5-parser — parser combinator with backtracking; Haiku drops ≥3 JSON5 features (unquoted reserved-word keys, hex literals, multiline continuation). (2) pkce-code-verifier — security protocol with crypto; Haiku uses base64 instead of base64url, omits timingSafeEqual. (3) two-phase-commit — distributed coordination FSM; Haiku breaks idempotency, NO-voter abort broadcast. (4) kahan-running-stats — numerical precision; Haiku uses population variance (n) instead of sample variance (n-1) and skips RangeError throwing. (5) token-bucket-rate-limiter — stateful time-based FSM with fake-timer oracle; Haiku omits edge cases (tryConsume(0), TypeError for negatives, capacity cap on refill). Per DEC-BENCH-METHODOLOGY-NEVER-SYNTHETIC-001: all oracles hand-authored, no LLM judges.

per_task_traps.json5-parser: Haiku commonly omits ≥3 features: unquoted reserved-word keys (for, if), hex literals (0xCAFE), line-continuation in strings, or implements block comments incorrectly; failing ≥30% of oracle cases
per_task_traps.pkce-code-verifier: Haiku uses standard base64 (contains +//) not base64url (-/_), omits = stripping, skips timingSafeEqual — fails RFC 7636 test vector and multiple oracle cases
per_task_traps.two-phase-commit: Haiku breaks idempotency (double-commit throws), fails to abort YES-voters when any-NO, gets state machine transitions wrong from non-idle states
per_task_traps.kahan-running-stats: Haiku uses population variance (divides by n) not sample variance (divides by n-1, Bessel's correction) — critical oracle discriminator; also omits RangeError type specificity
per_task_traps.token-bucket-rate-limiter: Haiku omits tryConsume(0)/consume(0) edge cases, skips TypeError for negative inputs, uses naive refill without capping at capacity

note: Task manifest for B4-tokens-v3 two-phase hypothesis matrix. SHA-256 hashes content-address each prompt file. Harness verifies on startup and aborts on drift.

tasks.description: Parse JSON5 (superset of JSON): single-quoted strings, unquoted keys, trailing commas, comments, hex numbers, Infinity/NaN, leading/trailing decimal, line continuation.
tasks.adversarial_framing: Parser combinator task. Haiku typically drops ≥3 JSON5 features, most commonly: unquoted reserved-word keys ({for:1}), hex literals (0xFF), line-continuation escapes in strings, or misimplements block comment termination.

tasks.description: Implement PKCE (RFC 7636): generateCodeVerifier (crypto.randomBytes, base64url), computeCodeChallenge (SHA-256 + base64url), verifyPkce (timingSafeEqual).
tasks.adversarial_framing: Security protocol with RFC 7636 test vector. Haiku typically uses standard base64 (with +/ chars) instead of base64url (-_ chars, no padding), fails the test vector, and omits timingSafeEqual for constant-time comparison.

tasks.description: Synchronous 2PC coordinator: Participant (prepare/commit/abort FSM) + Coordinator (run protocol, all-YES commit, any-NO abort all).
tasks.adversarial_framing: Distributed coordination FSM. Haiku commonly: breaks idempotency (double-commit throws instead of no-op), fails to call abort() on YES-voters when another participant votes NO, gets coordinator state transitions wrong.

tasks.description: Streaming statistics with Kahan compensated sum + Welford running variance (sample variance, n-1 denominator) + min/max + RangeError on empty.
tasks.adversarial_framing: Numerical precision + statistics correctness. Critical discriminator: sample variance (n-1) vs population variance (n). Haiku uses n denominator, failing [1,3]→2.0 and [1,2,3]→1.0 oracle cases. Also omits RangeError (uses Error) and misses empty-set throws.

tasks.description: Token bucket rate limiter: tryConsume/consume with time-based refill (Date.now()), capacity cap, RateLimitError with available/requested fields, reset().
tasks.adversarial_framing: Stateful time-based FSM with fake-timer oracle. Haiku commonly omits: tryConsume(0)/consume(0) always-true contract, TypeError for negative token requests, capacity cap on refill overshoot, RateLimitError.available and .requested properties.
